10496.history: Grounded at Baliquias Bay, Mindoro, Philippine Islands in position 13º50'N, 120º10'E. Destroyed to prevent capture.

10500.history: Mistakenly sunk by the US destroyer USS Conyngham and the US destroyer escort USS Lough near Talin Point, Luzon, Philippines in position 13º55'N, 120º36'E.

10502.history: Mistakenly sunk by the US destroyer USS Conyngham and the US destroyer escort USS Lough near Talin Point, Luzon, Philippines in position 13º55'N, 120º36'E.

10508.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 1. Sunk on 28 February 1943 while on passage to Great Britain aboard the American merchant vessel Wade Hampton. The Wade Hampton was torpedoed and sunk in the North Atlantic in position 59.49N, 34.43W by the German submarine U-405.

10509.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 3. Sunk on 28 February 1943 while on passage to Great Britain aboard the American merchant vessel Wade Hampton. The Wade Hampton was torpedoed and sunk in the North Atlantic in position 59.49N, 34.43W by the German submarine U-405.

10510.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 2. Transported to the Soviet Union and renamed TKA 201.

10511.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 5. Transported to the Soviet Union and renamed TKA 202.

10512.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 4. Due to the cancellation of convoys to Russia in the spring of 1943, this ship ended up in Great Britain and was incorporated into the Royal Navy in April 1943 as MTB 419.

10513.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 6. Due to the cancellation of convoys to Russia in the spring of 1943, this ship ended up in Great Britain and was incorporated into the Royal Navy in April 1943 as MTB 420.

10517.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 10. Due to the cancellation of convoys to Russia in the spring of 1943, this ship ended up in Great Britain and was incorporated into the Royal Navy in April 1943 as MTB 423.

10518.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 11. Due to the cancellation of convoys to Russia in the spring of 1943, this ship ended up in Great Britain and was incorporated into the Royal Navy in April 1943 as target tower CT 16.

10519.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union on 15 February 1943 being renamed RPT 12. Due to the cancellation of convoys to Russia in the spring of 1943, this ship ended up in Great Britain and was incorporated into the Royal Navy in April 1943 as target tower CT 17.

10521.history: Sunk in a collision with an unknown object off Newport, Rhode Island in position 41º23'N, 71º01'W.

10522.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy in 1944 being renamed MGB 181. Transferred by the British to Yugoslavia in August 1945.

10523.history: Mined and sunk off southern France in the Gulf of Frejus in position 43º23'N, 06º43'E.

10524.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy in 1944 being renamed MGB 189. Transferred by the British to Yugoslavia in August 1945.

10539.history: Mined and sunk off southern France in the Gulf of Frejus in position 43º23'N, 06º43'E.

10540.history: Heavily damaged in a storm near Attu, Aleutian Islands on 14 September 1943 and scrapped.

10561.history: Destroyed by fire at Lambu Lambu Cove, Vella Lavella, Solomon Islands in position 07º42'S, 156º47'E.

10575.history: Grounded in the Empress Augusta Bay, Solomon Islands and sunk by Japanese shore batteries in position 06º30'S, 155º10'E.

10576.history: Sunk of the southeastern coast of Bougainville in position 06º38'S, 156º01'E by gunfire from Japanese barges and Japanese coastal batteries.

10577.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union in November 1943 being renamed TK 202.

10578.history: Transferred to the Soviet Union in November 1943 being renamed TK 203.

10605.history: Sunk in a collision with USS PT 282 off Bougainville, Solomon Islands in position 05º30'S, 154º15'E.

10606.history: Mistakenly sunk by gunfire from the US destroyer USS Guest off Bougainville, Solomon Islands in position 06º27'S, 155º08'E.

10612.history: Sunk by a Japanese kamikaze aircraft off Mindoro, Philippines in position 12º19'N, 121º05'E.

10613.history: Heavily damaged on 7 November 1944 by an accidental explosion at the forward base of Mios Woendi, Biak island in position 01.15S, 136.23E. Repairs were deemed impossible and she was laid up as a constructive total loss (CTL). Destroyed on 15 August 1945.

10623.history: PT 311 (Lt.(jg) Brenton Welles Creelman, USNR) was mined and sunk south-south-west of La Spezia, Italy in position 43°51'N, 09°37'E.
commanders: Ens. Brenton Wellis Creelman, USNR (25 Jan 1944 - 18 Nov 1944 (+))
